observations.recommendation:
1.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the journal
entries in the transfer of PPE accounts and its related Accumulated
Depreciation from the Trust Fund to General Fund books.

2.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw an adjusting
entry on the affected accounts and record all subsequent fund transfers
from National Government Agencies for the implementation of specific
programs or projects to “Due to National Government Agencies”
account.

3.12. We recommended that the:
3.12.1. Municipal Accountant (a) designate at least one personnel in
the Accounting Office to obtain the bank statements CY 2025
to present and reconcile them with the Accounting Records
and submit the resulting Bank Reconciliation Statements to the
Audit Team; (b) make the necessary adjustments in the setup
of the subsidiary ledgers to ensure that they align with the
existing bank accounts; and (c) prepare adjusting journal
entries based on the results of the reconciliation, if necessary;
and
3.12.2. Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Accounting Office to
reconcile their records, ensuring both departments are aligned
and reflect the same cash positions.

4.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose fully the
details of the Loans Payable in the Notes to Financial Statements
financial statements.

5.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Analyze the Loans Payable and the related interest to ensure proper
recognition of the current and non-current portions; and
b. Present properly the current and non-current portion of the Loans
Payable, thereby, ensuring that the Financial Statements are free
from inaccurate or misleading information.

6.13. We recommended that the:
6.13.1. Municipal Accountant:
a. Revert the entry made to derecognize the aforementioned
PPE Items; and
b. Disclose to the Notes to Financial Statements these PPE
Items, with additional information on the status of the
request for authority for derecognition, if the same have not
been dropped from the books at year-end.

6.13.2. Municipal Mayor with the assistance of the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal GSO – Designate:
a. Submit a request for authority to derecognize the non-
existing/missing PPEs to the Audit Team together with the
following supporting documents:
• List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs and their carrying
values certified by the Heads of the Property Unit and
Accounting Unit and approved by the Head of the
Agency;
• Certification by the Head of the Accounting Unit that the
non-existing/missing PPEs had already exceeded their
estimated useful lives;
• Certified copy of the investigation conducted pursuant
to Item 7.9 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. Submit the documentation of the One-Time Cleansing of
PPEs including the Physical Inventory Plan, Inventory
Count Forms, and other schedules required under COA
Circular No. 2020-006 for verification of the Audit Team.

7.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the
implementing guidelines of COA Circular No. 2024-006, particularly
regarding the accounting treatment of tangible items below the new
capitalization threshold.

8.6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the liquidation
of cash advances to Special Disbursing Officers and Officers and
Employees granted in previous year to Prior Period Adjustment
account, if the said liquidation took place in the ensuing year, to fairly
present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the management agreed to create an Inventory Committee to conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE by the adhering the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and file the request for authority to derecognize non-existing/ missing PPEs to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/ or the Supervising Auditor (SA), if any; and agreed to direct the General Service Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update timely the property cards and PPE ledger cards, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to remove all tangible items with acquisition
costs below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 from the PPE
accounts together with their respective accumulated depreciation.
Moreover, the new capitalization threshold under COA Circular No. 2022-
004 should be considered in determining whether or not to record newly
acquired goods under the PPE accounts.

2.7. We recommended and the Supply Officer agreed to, in coordination with
the Municipal Account, hasten the process of disposing the
unserviceable properties so that the same may be appropriately dropped
from the books of accounts which in turn will provide for a more accurate
balance of the PPE accounts.

3.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record all
heavy equipment such as Forklift, Grader, Backhoe, Dump Trucks, and
the like, although used in disaster response and rescue, under the
“Construction and Heavy Equipment” account in accordance with
Section 5.1.7 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

4.9. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the grant
and liquidation of cash advances for the payment of unpaid wages in the
previous year in accordance with the applicable accounting rules and
regulation in order to fairly present the expenses in the financial statements.

5.10. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and
submit the bank reconciliation statements for Calendar Year 2023 by
delegating the tasks to regular accounting personnel to ensure their
timely submission in future periods.

6.10. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an
Appraisal Committee to aid the Inventory Committee in the
implementation of the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No.
2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances in order to
address the issues that are preventing the reconciliation of records
between the concerned offices and to establish accurate balances of the
PPE accounts that are to be presented in the financial statements.

7.6. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Administrator agreed to ensure that the disbursement vouchers
pertaining to the release of Confidential Fund, together with the required
transmittal letter and supporting documents, are submitted to the COA
Audit Team Leader within seven calendar days after the release of the
related checks pursuant to Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND
Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

8.9. We recommended and (i) The MDRRM Officer agreed to prepare the
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the prescribed format,
certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit to the Office of
the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, copy
furnish the OCD and DILG-LGOO along with the copy of LDRRMFIP; and
(ii) The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council agreed
to identify DRRM related PPAs to be charged against prior years’ LDRRMF
unexpended balances and include the same in the LDRRMFIP under a
separate caption.

9.11. We recommended and: (a) The Municipal Treasurer agreed to transfer the
unexpended balances of the LDRRMF for MOOE and QRF aggregating
₱3,185,193.99 to the Special Trust Fund bank account; (b) The Municipal
Budget Officer agreed to prepare and maintain the RAAOs for the
LDRRMF in a timely manner; and (c) The Municipal Accountant agreed to
record the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF under the “Trust
Liabilities - Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” account
once the transfer to the Special Trust Fund bank account is made and
prepare Subsidiary Ledgers for the said balance as well as report the
same in the financial statements in the prescribed manner.

10.10. We recommended and: (i) The municipal officials and employees agreed
to charge the unforeseeable and non-recurring expenses to the Petty
Cash Fund and discontinue the practice of reimbursing transactions that
did not undergo the prescribed procurement process; and (ii) The Bids
and Awards Committee agreed to review and update the existing
procurement plan to include specific strategies and procurement
methods for managing urgent repairs, maintenance, and time-sensitive
activities, based on historical data.

11.8. We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to develop
a mechanism that would hasten the preparation of contract documents in
order to ensure compliance with the prescribed period of its submission.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct (a) the Municipal Accountant to bring the RPT and balance of the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET as of December 31, 2022 to its correct amounts using the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2022, adjust the RPT-Basic/SET and Due to LGUs accounts and record the succeeding RPT and SET transactions as prescribed by Sections 36 and 93 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; (b) the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Accountant at the beginning of the year of the Certified List Tax Collectibles for the current year and an updated Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies before the close of accounting books; and (c) both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to perform periodic reconciliation of the RPT/SET Receivables as well as Deferred RPT/SET.

observations.recommendation: 1.8	We recommended that Management regularly conduct monthly and quarterly reconciliations of their books of accounts and prepare report of reconciliation and copy furnish the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

2.8	We recommended that Management take option to request for snap shots from the bank every end of the month. These documents could be used in lieu of the bank statements to facilitate timely preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements to support Management’s assertion on the existence and accuracy of the reported valuation of Cash in Bank-LCCA in the financial statements. Likewise, fast-track the reconciliation of other bank accounts so as to catch-up with the requirements for the current year.

3.12	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accounting Office a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis in setting up at beginning of the year. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year using the certified list of taxpayers. Also, require the Municipal Assessor to update the list of taxpayers duly certified indicating the amount and collectible for the year 2021 and thereafter and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer.

4.11	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Team to conduct reconciliation of balances. For PPE items without immediately identifiable cost, the Appraisal Committee determines the assessed or appraised value of an item to be the unit value. In addition, follow the procedures on the one-time cleansing of PPE account prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020. Further, we enjoin the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for every PPE class using the prescribed template/format accordingly.

5.6	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation expenses consistent to all depreciable assets as required under IPSAS 17. Further, make necessary adjustments to correct the noted deficiency.

6.8	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to take up loans’ payable on the approved loans in the books of accounts for CY 2021 and provide sufficient disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements regarding the terms and conditions in relation to the term loan agreement.

7.10	We recommended that Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the concerned Accountable Officers requiring them to immediately liquidate and render account for their long overdue cash advances or refund in favor of the government the unliquidated amount, if applicable.

8.13	We recommended that Management enhance effectively and efficiently on the procurement planning in the utilization of the LDRRMIP/F of PPAs for the year it was budgeted for the welfare of its constituent. Likewise, require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to apply augmentation appropriately in accordance with Article 454 of AO No. 270. Further, the LDRRM Officer refrain from using the LDRRMF to expenses that do not partake to any of the thematic area nor the related PPA enumerated in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1. Likewise, refrain from disbursing public funds without any appropriation.

9.8	We recommended that Management direct the: (1) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to fully transfer the unutilized balances of LDRRMF for                       CY 2021 and prior years from the General Fund to a Special Trust Fund account; (2) Ensure that the Municipal Accountant observe the accounting for the unexpended LDRRMF balance and the required disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; and (3) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization in the prescribed format, certify its correctness and submit to the Auditor in the manner prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002.

10.12	We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to base all procurements with the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) so as not to resort to splitting of requisitions and to conduct competitive bidding if procurement exceeded the threshold amount. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant not to process the transactions if not supported with complete documents.

11.8	We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to properly control/regulate the issuance of diesel/gasoline through a properly accomplished trip tickets and withdrawal slip. Ensure that all trip tickets were properly filled out with all necessary information and authorized for official purpose only. Moreover, refrain from issuing fuel withdrawal slips to private vehicles or those that cannot be identified as government vehicles. Likewise, mark its owned vehicles with “For Official Use Only” as well as use government plates. Further, require all drivers concerned to prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travel supported with trip tickets and the Supply/Property Officer prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption. Accordingly, submit these reports to the office of the Auditor on a monthly basis. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate compliance with the reports for submission to the auditor for monitoring and verifying fuel consumption on post audit basis.

12.7	We recommended that Management direct the concerned Officer to furnish the COA Audit Team Leader with copies of contracts, Purchase Orders and all related supporting documents thereto within five (5) after its perfection for evaluation and review. Also, submit the Notices of Deliveries to the Auditor within the prescribed period to conduct timely inspection of deliveries on a sampling basis pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 8, 1995.

13.5	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to (1) prepare and maintain Journals and Ledgers, (2) reclassify unusual accounts to appropriate account titles under the Revised Chart of Accounts, and (3) maintain separate books of accounts and financial reports for Special Accounts in the General Fund and Trust Fund.

14.8	We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure that GAD plans have sufficient budget to fund projects, programs and activities that are GAD-related. If GAD attribution is opted, ensure that gender analysis is conducted through administration of HGDG tool so that only those programs and projects can be appropriately attributed to GAD budget. Likewise, incorporate the GAD Plan and Budget in agency’s annual budget regardless of fund allocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on the foregoing-stated PPEs to correctly present their balances in the financial statements.  Maintenance of records on the PPEs must be done; and conduct appraisal, through an appraisal committee, the value and useful life of the subject PPEs as basis for the computation of depreciation expense in case historical data of said PPEs are no longer available.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents with covering transmittal letter pertaining to the cash advances granted for confidential fund in a manner prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001.  Strictly adhere to the intent of the provisions enshrined in the said Joint Circular.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of procuring goods and services through reimbursements scheme.  Reimbursements, as a mode of procurement, should not be allowed in the purchase of goods and services; Conduct competitive bidding in all its procurement as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184. Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow.  Submit to the Auditor justification / explanation on the occurrence of the irregularities; strictly adhere to the provision of Section 10 of R.A. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the missing DVs shown in the schedule.   Adhere to Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 44 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the submission of DVs and its supporting documents to the Auditor.

We recommended and Management agreed to utilize the loan proceeds for infrastructure and other socio-economic development projects and to stabilize local finances as stated in Sections 296 of R.A. No. 7160.The Agency must ensure that the purpose of contracting loans is being served; submit to the Auditor justification/comment on the occurrence of the above irregularities.   Strictly adhere to Sections 296 and 3 of R.A. No. 7160 and E.O. No. 723, respectively, in entering into credit finances.

We recommended and Management agreed to provide the Inventory Committee sufficient manpower compliment and office equipment to be used in the conduct of annual physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; advise the Storekeeper to maintain and update Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records; and adhere to provisions on property management set forth in the above-quoted regulations and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and the LGU agreed to execute and submit to COA the service contracts of the job orders.  Ensure that duties and responsibilities to be performed are outlined in the contract, a copy of which must be issued to and received by the employee concerned; seek SB authority/resolution allowing the Municipal Mayor to incur obligation in paying the wages of the job orders; and revised the rates given to the job orders, from fixed monthly rate to hourly, daily or piecework rate basis.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required financial statements and reports, as well as the lacking/missing disbursement vouchers to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and Chapter 4 of NGAS Manual for LGU.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring goods and services through reimbursements scheme; support the disbursements with complete documents to establish existence, validity and propriety of the payments; disbursement vouchers and supporting documents must be completely and properly filled up so as to provide clear information of the transaction; and strictly adhere to the provision of Section 10 of R.A. 9184 when procuring goods, services or infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Accountant agreed to request for snap shots from
the bank every end of the month. These documents could be used in lieu of the
bank statements to facilitate timely preparation and submission of Bank
Reconciliation Statements to support Management’s assertion on the existence
and accuracy of the reported valuation of Cash in Bank - LCCA in the financial
statements.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct annual
physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report
thereon to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover,
direct the GSO and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain Property
Cards and PPE Ledgers Cards, respectively and ensure that reconciliation of their
records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements.

We recommended and the Accounting Unit agreed to:

a. Prepare the adjusting journal entries as follows:
Dr: Prior Year Adjustment P1,352,148.75
Cr: Leave Benefits payable P1,352,148.75

b. From then on, require the HRMO in charge to submit to the
Accounting Unit the periodic report on accumulated leave balances to
facilitate the recognition of the cost of the compensated absences
incurred for the current year;

c. Gradually set up the necessary funds for terminal leave benefits of
employees by accruing the monthly actual earned leave benefits of its
personnel; and

d. Henceforth, periodically record in the books of accounts the
compensated absences earned and ensure that funds are set up for the
timely and expeditious release of the retirement pay in accordance
with Paragraph 11 of the Philippine Accounting Standards (PAS) 19
and Section 1 of Republic Act No. 101541 respectively.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to furnish the
Municipal Accountant the duly certified list of taxpayers together with the
amount due and collectible and advise the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV
based on the said list in order to establish RPT/SET receivables at the beginning
of the year.

We recommended and the Management agreed to meticulously screen any
loan application of its employees, especially those offered by financial
institutions aside from the GSIS and PAG-IBIG to ensure that their net take home
pay does not fall below the required minimum take home pay provided by law.

We recommended and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer agreed to timely
update its cashbooks and conduct reconciliation of the account balances per
Cashbook and per General Ledger of the Accounting Office quarterly or as often
as needed. Identify the items that need adjustments and record them
appropriately in the books to ascertain accuracy of the reported cash balances.

We recommended and the Solid Waste Management Board agreed to provide
necessary support to the MENRO-Designate to ensure approval of the Solid
Waste Management Plan and the implementation of the solid waste programs,
projects and activities.

1.8 We recommended for Management to plan and conduct physical count of
the Municipality’s properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit
Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE pursuant to Section 124 of
NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain
and update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each
category of assets; advise the Storekeeper to maintain and update
Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment, and to
reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records; direct the
Storekeeper to exert more effort in identifying these unserviceable
properties and to trace them in records ready for their disposal; and
adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in the above-
quoted regulations and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

2.8 We recommended that Management:
2.8.1 Instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the adjusting journal
entries as follows:
Dr: Retained Earnings/(Deficit) P6,317,448.38
Cr: Leave Benefits payable P6,317,448.38
2.8.2 From then on, require the HRMO in charge to submit to the
Accounting Unit the periodic report on accumulated leave balances
to facilitate the recognition of the cost of the compensated absences
incurred for the current year.
2.8.3 Set up slowly the necessary funds for terminal leave benefits of
employees by accruing the monthly actual earned leave benefits of
its personnel.
2.8.4 Henceforth, instruct the Accounting Unit to periodically record in
the books of accounts the compensated absences earned and ensure
that funds is set up for the timely and expeditious release of the
retirement pay in accordance with Paragraph 11 of the Philippine
Accountings Standards (PAS) 19 and Section 1 of Republic Act No.
101541 respectively.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall;
3.8.1 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
fully transfer the unutilized balances of LDRRMF for CY 2018 and
its prior years from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund in
the total amount of P5,581,355.87.
3.8.2 Ensure that the Municipal Accountant shall observe the
requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012 and Item 6 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013
specifically the guidelines on the accounting and reporting of
LDRRMF to ascertain that the financial information on LDRRMF
are taken up appropriately in the books and properly disclosed in
the Financial Statements.

4.5 With the deficiencies discussed, we recommended for Management to:
4.5.1 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
ensure that funds transferred to LDRRMF includes not only 5%
from the IRA but also from other local sources of income;
4.5.2 Require the MDRRMO to prepare the monthly Utilization Report
duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by
the Municipal Accountant.

5.5 We recommended for the Management to instruct the Cashier or the
Municipal Treasurer that henceforth, all paid disbursement vouchers and
its supporting documents be properly stamped “PAID” and dated as to
payment to preclude double payment of the same transactions.

6.6 We reiterated our recommendation for the Management to strictly
instruct the Accounting Office to submit the monthly Trial Balances and
Financial Statements within the prescribed period required under Section
41 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 (PD 1445), Section 347 of Local
Government Code of 1991 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 and remind
the concerned official or employee that failure to comply shall cause the
automatic suspension of the payment of his/her salary and other
emoluments until he/she shall have complied therewith and that violation
of these provisions for at least three times shall subject the offender to
administrative disciplinary action.

7.7 We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Point System
to prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report to DILG
Regional Office and furnish the same to the Office of the Auditor.

8.9 We recommend that Management instruct the GAD Focal Point to refrain
from including financial assistance to individuals in crisis in the GAD Plan
and Budget without conducting appropriate gender analysis and/or
gender assessment and ensure that the planned GAD programs and
activities are truly gender-responsive.

9.8 We recommended that Management see to it that PAPs proposed to be
undertaken during the budget year are implemented or completed in the
same year in order to attain its objective in providing timely socio-
economic benefits to its constituents, effect implementation timelines
considering the available resources to serve as guide in monitoring the
status of each project and enhance monitoring activities in order to
achieve the desired results/ outputs at different stages of project
implementation.

10.10 We recommended for the management to fast-track the implementation of
the programmed SWM projects under 20% DF in order to address the
current risks/ hazards brought by maintaining an open dumpsite and
proceed with the appropriate closure and rehabilitation procedures as
planned for the said dumpsite located at Km. 3, Barangay Sta Fe.

observations.recommendation:
1.34  We recommended and Management agreed to:

           1.34.1      Stop the granting of  additional  cash  advances  to
                 officials and employees with outstanding balances;


           1.34.2       Stop  granting  cash  advances  other  than  travel
                 expenses to an elective official of the Municipality;


           1.34.3      Stop the practice  of  granting  cash  advances  for
                 expenses  other  that  travel  expenses  to  officials  and
                 employees of the Municipality who are not  bonded  and  not
                 designated as special disbursing officers;


           1.34.4      Strictly enforce the liquidation and  collection  of
                 the above stated unliquidated cash advances;
           1.34.5       The  Municipal  Accountant  shall  demand  for  the
                 liquidation of the above stated unliquidated cash advances;


           1.34.6      In the subsequent granting of cash advances  and  in
                 order to prevent  the  accumulation  of  unliquidated  cash
                 advances, the Municipal Accountant shall  send  within  ten
                 (10) days before the  expiration  of  the  30  days  period
                 written  notices/reminders  and   letters   enjoining   the
                 officers and  employees  to  liquidate  their  travel  cash
                 advances.

2.8     We recommended  and  Management  agreed  to  constitute  a  new
    inventory committee/team and designate new members thereof.  This  team
    will spearhead, plan and conduct physical count of  the  Municipality’s
    properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team  the  Report  on
    Physical Count of PPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual  for  LGU;
    instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the  Property,
    Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category  of  assets;  advise
    the Storekeeper to maintain and update Property Cards per  category  of
    property, plant and equipment, and to reconcile balances  thereof  with
    that of the accounting records; direct the Storekeeper  to  exert  more
    effort in identifying these unserviceable properties and to trace  them
    in records ready for their disposal; and adhere to  the  provisions  on
    property management set forth in the above-quoted regulations and other
    pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

3.8   We recommended that the Management shall  record  cash  advances
      for confidential expenses as Advances to  Special  Disbursing  Officer
      and  draw  the  suggested  adjusting  journal  below  to  correct  the
      misstatement of the financial statements:


            Advances to Special Disbursing Officer       1,500,000.00
                  Prior Period Adjustment                      1,500,000.00


      3.9    Further,  the  Municipal  Accountant  shall  record  only   the
      liquidation of the cash advances for confidential fund  in  the  books
      within five days after the receipt of the copy of the  transmittal  of
      required documents duly acknowledged by  the  ICFAU.  She  shall  then
      submit the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the COA  Audit
      Team Leader (ATL).

4.12  We recommended and Management agreed with the following:

           4.12.1      Direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  ensure  that
                 financial transactions are properly recognized and recorded
                 in accordance with Sections4(8) and 122 of PD No. 1445  and
                 COA Circular Nos. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and  2016-
                 005;

           4.12.2      The Municipal Accountant shall effect the  suggested
                 adjusting journal entries in  their  book  of  accounts  to
                 correct  the  erroneous  recording  of  accounts  resulting
                 misstatements of the FS:


                     Due from LGUs                     872,000.00
                         Government Equity                     872,000.00
                    (To correct entry made for Financial Assistance  granted
                    for CY 2017)
           4.12.3      Require the  recipient  LGU-Barangays  to  submit  a
                 liquidation  report  duly  verified  by  the  COA  resident
                 auditor to the accounting office.

5.26  We recommended and Management agreed to:

           5.26.1      Require the barangays  to  accomplish  all  required
                 documents including liquidation reports with the supporting
                 SCI and SCP after  implementation  of  their  developmental
                 projects and return any unutilized fund balance;


           5.26.2      Prepare budget based on  the  duly  approved  Annual
                 Investment  Plan  indicating  the   details   (e.g.   exact
                 location, amount of the project based on POW) of each  PPAs
                 and identify thereof the outputs;


           5.26.3       Instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  make   an
                 adjusting entry for the improper recognition of  the  funds
                 transferred to the barangays by debiting Due From LGUs  (1-
                 03-03-030) and crediting Prior Period Adjustment  (3-01-01-
                 020) in the amount of P2,950,000.00; and


           5.26.4      Stop the practice of utilizing the 20%  DF  to  PPAs
                 that are not related to  capital  expenditures.  Prioritize
                 the PPAs that contribute to  the  attainment  of  desirable
                 socio-economic  development  and  environmental  management
                 outcomes of the LGU.


           5.26.5      Utilization of appropriations presented in lump  sum
                 appropriations and in generic terms shall be supported with
                 sanggunian authorization.

6.6   We recommended and Management agreed to instruct  the  Municipal
      Accountant to prepare the suggested adjusting entry  shown  below  and
      prepare the  necessary  subsidiary  ledger  supported  with  equipment
      ledgers in compliance with Section 4(8) of PD 1445  and  COA  Circular
      No. 2012-002:


            Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment           85,096.05
                                                                 Watercrafts
                               85,096.05

                  (To correct entry made for recording fabrication of 1 unit
      pump boat)

observations.recommendation:
1.5     We recommended and Management agreed to  direct  the  Municipal
    Accountant to prepare and submit without delay the Bank  Reconciliation
    Statements of all the funds/bank accounts maintained by the former with
    the authorized depository banks.

2.6     We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the  Municipal
    Accountant to set up RPT/SET receivables using the  list  of  taxpayers
    together with the amount due and collectible based on the Real Property
    Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card  at  the  beginning  of  the
    year. Further, advice the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the accounting
    office of the said list at the earliest possible and adhere to Sections
    246 and 20 of R. A. No. 7160 and NGAS for LGU, Volume I,  respectively,
    in setting up the tax receivable accounts to effectively monitor  their
    recording and disposition.

3.7     We recommended  and  Management  agreed  to  constitute  a  new
    inventory committee/team and designate new members thereof.  This  team
    will spearhead, plan and conduct physical count of  the  Municipality’s
    properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team  the  Report  on
    Physical Count of PPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual  for  LGU;
    instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the  Property,
    Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category  of  assets;  advise
    the Storekeeper to maintain and update Property Cards per  category  of
    property, plant and equipment, and to reconcile balances  thereof  with
    that of the accounting records; direct the Storekeeper  to  exert  more
    effort in identifying these unserviceable properties and to trace  them
    in records ready for their disposal; and adhere to  the  provisions  on
    property management set forth in the above-quoted regulations and other
    pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

4.7     We recommended and Management agreed to  immediately  designate
    an  employee  who  shall  be  responsible  to  facilitate  the   LTO
    registration and insurance of all motor vehicles owned by  the  Parish
    Municipality.  Apply and/or renew the registration of all serviceable
    motor vehicles of the municipality with LTO and contracts of  insurance
    with the GSIS to avoid additional  expenses  in  terms  of  penalties
    and payment of damages to third party liabilities in  case  of  vehicular
    accidents.

5.5     We recommended and Management agreed to  instruct  the  GSO  to
    prepare  IIRUP  for  approval  by  the  management;  the  Committee  on
    Appraisal of  Property  appraises  those  properties  and  request  the
    disposal of the same pursuant to COA rules and regulations  to  prevent
    further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.

7.7     We recommended and  the  Management   agreed  to  instruct  the
    Municipal Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  transfer  the
    unutilized balances of LDRRMF for CYs 2015 and 2016  from  the  General
    Fund to the Special Trust Fund in the total amount of P4,142,442.66  in
    accordance with Item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12,
    2012 and Item 6 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No.
    2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.


    7.8      Further,  we  recommended  and  Management  agreed  that   the
    Municipal Accountant shall observe the requirements set forth under COA
    Circular No. 2012-002 specifically the guidelines on the accounting and
    reporting of LDRRMF to ascertain that  the  financial  information  on
    LDRRMF are taken up appropriately in the books and  properly  disclosed
    in the Financial Statements.

8.12    We  recommend  and  Management  agreed  to  require  the  Local
    Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) to  prepare  and
    submit to the Municipal Accounting, Budget and COA  Offices  the  Local
    Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan  (LDRRMFIP)
    which shall present the 30 percent allocation for Quick  Response  Fund
    in lump-sum and the 70  percent  allocation  for  disaster  mitigation,
    prevention and preparedness with details as to  priority  projects  and
    activities to be implemented in the ensuing year  pursuant to Section
    12( c) and 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

9.6     We recommend that Management strictly comply with Section 5  of
    RA 10121  to  keep  the  public  informed  on  the  implementation  and
    utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to the transparency and full  disclosure
    policy of the  government.   Further,  the  LDRRMO  and  the  Municipal
    Accountant  shall  prepare  and  submit  the  Report  on  Sources   and
    Utilization of LDRRMF to the COA Auditor as required in Item  5.1.5  of
    COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

10.7    We recommend that Management immediately issue a check from 20%
    Development Fund in the amount P400,000.00  and  deposit  the  same  in
    favor to LDRRMF to replenish the erroneously disbursed fund.

11.4 We recommend that Management require the BAC,  including  the  BAC
    Secretariat and the Technical Working Group, to  submit  to  the  Audit
    Team the lacking documents and to explain to the latter the presence of
    procurement  deficiencies/irregularities.  Adhere  strictly  with   the
    provisions of R.A. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR,  COA  Circular  Nos.
    2009-001 and 2012-001 on the procurement of goods,  infrastructure  and
    services.

13.10   We recommend that the Local School Board convene  annually  and
    prepare the required annual school board budget identifying therein the
    priority programs/projects/activities chargeable to  Special  Education
    Fund in accordance with the provisions of Sections 98 (b)  (3)  and  99
    (a) and 100 of Republic Act No. 7160.


    13.11   Further, stop the practice of disbursing funds without  budget.
    Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the  Treasurer  to  refrain  from
    processing claims against Special Education Fund unless there is  an
    approved annual school board  budget.  When  such  shall  be  prepared,
    disbursements   should   always    be    based    on    the    priority
    programs/projects/activities identified from it.

14.9    We recommend that (1) the Sangguniang Bayan shall  prepare  and
    approve the needed resolution to facilitate the speedy closure of their
    PNB account and submit a copy of such to the  Management  and  the  COA
    office; and (2) Management shall effect the  immediate  termination  of
    its depository account in the PNB-Surigao Branch, transfer the  balance
    of the fund to the general fund account maintained in LBP and submit to
    the audit team a copy of the communication made to the bank as well  as
    the proof that said account is already closed.

15.10   We recommend that Management require the  Municipal  Accountant
    and Treasurer to submit the Disbursement  Vouchers,  Official  Receipts
    and reports as of December 31, 2016  due  for  submission  to  COA  and
    ensure that the disbursement vouchers are duly supported  and  properly
    documented. A report shall be prepared and submitted to the COA by  the
    Municipal Treasurer stating therein  that  there  are  still  unclaimed
    checks and unendorsed disbursement vouchers under its custody. Strictly
    observe the deadline of submission of accounts and reports to COA.

16.10   We recommended and Management agreed to  strictly  monitor  the
    use of government vehicles and mark them with “For official  use  only”
    including pump boats. Direct the drivers and operators to  prepare  the
    monthly report of fuel consumption and summary of official  travel  for
    proper control and monitoring. Otherwise, failure to  comply  therewith
    shall cause the  disallowance  of  all  expenses  such  as  fuel,  oil,
    gasoline, spare parts and repairs in compliance with COA  Circular  No.
    75-7 and 77-61.

17.13   We recommend and Management agreed to:

            1. require the end-user offices to submit the PPMP  in  July  of
               the preceding budget year to the BAC Secretariat which  shall
               consolidate the  same  in  coordination  with  the  Municipal
               Budget  Officer  for  the  funding   allocation,   into   the
               Municipality’s consolidated APP for the  LCE’s  approval  and
               integration in the LCE’s executive budget;
            2. observe Sections 7.3.5 and 7.4 of the IRR, and other  related
               issuances in the APP subsequent updating/revision considering
               the approved appropriation; and

            3. undertake the bulk procurement based on the APP from the  PS-
               DBM, and if they are not available thereat, adopt the  public
               bidding as  the  general  rule  in  the  procurement  of  the
               supplies, materials  and  other  pertinent  MOOE  items,  and
               equipment procurement.

19.7    We recommended and Management agreed to submit to  the  Auditor
    the lacking documents mentioned in the attached  schedule.  All  claims
    shall always  be  supported  with  complete  documentation;  Faithfully
    adhere  to  all  laws  and  regulations  applicable   to   financial
    transactions  of    any    government    agency;    Submit     to     the
    Auditor justification/explanation  on  the  occurrence  of  the  above-noted
    irregularities.   Failure  to  comply  may  cause  the  suspension   or
    disallowance of the transactions.

20.5    We recommend and Management agreed to  instruct  the  Bids  and
    Awards Committee to  ensure  that  the  submitted  RFQs  have  complete
    details as to the identity of the bidders such as the printed name  and
    contact numbers and to strictly adhere to  the  provisions  of  RA  No.
    9184.

Payments shall only be made to claims with complete supporting documents.
